openmeter/entitlement/balanceworker.purpose: Kafka-driven worker that recalculates entitlement balances on lifecycle events (grant created/voided, entitlement created/deleted/reset, batched ingest) and publishes snapshot events downstream. Primary constraint: every recalculation path must pass the filter chain before any DB/ClickHouse access, and lifecycle handlers must publish a RecalculateEvent rather than calculating inline.

patterns.name: Two-stage recalculation via RecalculateEvent
patterns.description: Direct lifecycle handlers in worker.go eventHandler() publish events.RecalculateEvent to the balance-worker topic; a separate RecalculateEvent handler calls handleEntitlementEvent. Decouples fan-out retry from calculation retry.
patterns.example: w.opts.EventBus.Publish(ctx, events.RecalculateEvent{Entitlement: pkgmodels.NamespacedID{...}, SourceOperation: events.OperationTypeEntitlementCreated})

patterns.name: Filter-before-calculate
patterns.description: handleEntitlementEvent calls w.filters.IsNamespaceInScope then IsEntitlementInScope before fetching; filtered events return (nil, nil), not an error.
patterns.example: inScope, err := w.filters.IsNamespaceInScope(ctx, entitlementID.Namespace); if !inScope { return nil, nil }

patterns.name: handleOption functional options with mandatory WithEventAt
patterns.description: handleEntitlementEventOptions built via WithSource/WithEventAt/WithSourceOperation/WithRawIngestedEvents; opts.Validate() errors when eventAt.IsZero().
patterns.example: w.handleEntitlementEvent(ctx, id, WithSource(src), WithEventAt(event.ResetAt), WithSourceOperation(snapshot.ValueOperationReset))

patterns.name: PublishIfNoError for conditional publish
patterns.description: Handlers that may return a nil event without error use w.opts.EventBus.WithContext(ctx).PublishIfNoError so a filtered nil-event is neither published nor retried.
patterns.example: return w.opts.EventBus.WithContext(ctx).PublishIfNoError(w.handleEntitlementEvent(ctx, id, options...))

patterns.name: Options structs Validate() first in every constructor
patterns.description: WorkerOptions, RecalculatorOptions, EntitlementFiltersConfig implement Validate() via errors.Join; New/NewRecalculator call it first and return early so missing deps surface as startup errors.
patterns.example: if err := opts.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to validate worker options: %w", err) }

patterns.name: RecordLastCalculation after each snapshot (non-fatal)
patterns.description: After createSnapshotEvent, call w.filters.RecordLastCalculation; failure only logs a warning since it merely affects high-watermark dedup.
patterns.example: if err := w.filters.RecordLastCalculation(...); err != nil { w.opts.Logger.WarnContext(ctx, ...) }

patterns.name: AddHandler for post-construction extensibility
patterns.description: w.AddHandler appends extra batched-ingest GroupEventHandlers after construction (used by billing-worker); handlers must be idempotent since errors trigger retry.
patterns.example: worker.AddHandler(grouphandler.NewGroupEventHandler(func(ctx, *ingestevents.EventBatchedIngest) error { ... }))

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: WorkerOptions, Worker, New() (subscribes 3 Kafka topics), eventHandler() registering every NoPublishingHandler closure.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new event-type handlers must be registered inside grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler in eventHandler(); AddHandler is the only post-construction extension point.
Reset uses CurrentUsagePeriod.From as calculatedAt, not time.Now(). Deleted/expired entitlements emit a nil-Value delete snapshot, not an error. Always call in.Validate() in snapshotToEvent.

key_file_guides.role: Recalculator struct for batch jobs: ListInScopeEntitlements (paginated 20k/page), ProcessEntitlements, sendEntitlementEvent; LRU+TTL caches for feature & customerSubject.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DefaultIncludeDeletedDuration=24h keeps deleted entitlements in scope; the paginated loop is intentional (Ent IN-subqueries break on large sets).

key_file_guides.role: handleBatchedIngestEvent: queries Repo for affected entitlements, checks activity-period overlap, publishes a RecalculateEvent per affected entitlement.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Deleted entitlements are explicitly skipped; activity period uses min(ActiveTo, DeletedAt) as upper bound via GetEntitlementActivityPeriod.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementFilters wraps []NamedFilter (NotificationsFilter + HighWatermarkCache) with OTel counters; RecordLastCalculation type-asserts CalculationTimeRecorder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Filter chain short-circuits on first false; only filters implementing CalculationTimeRecorder receive RecordLastCalculation.

key_file_guides.role: BalanceWorkerRepository interface + ListAffectedEntitlementsResponse; GetEntitlementActivityPeriod computes the valid event window.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ActiveFrom wins over CreatedAt; when both ActiveTo and DeletedAt present, the earlier wins as upper bound (lo.MinBy).

key_file_guides.role: resolveCustomerAndSubject helper shared by Worker and Recalculator; Subject may be nil for customers without usage attribution.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Nil UsageAttribution or no subject keys returns (customer, nil, nil); a missing subject row falls back to a synthetic Subject with just the key.

anti_patterns: Publishing a snapshot event directly from a lifecycle handler instead of going through RecalculateEvent — breaks two-stage dedup and the filter pipeline
Calling handleEntitlementEvent without WithEventAt — eventAt.IsZero() is a hard validation error
Adding DB or external-service calls to filter implementations without LRU+TTL caching — IsNamespaceInScope/IsEntitlementInScope run per-event and saturate downstream services
Registering new event-type handlers outside the grouphandler.NewNoPublishingHandler block in eventHandler() — they are never called
Using time.Now() as calculatedAt for reset operations — reset snapshots must use CurrentUsagePeriod.From

decisions.decision: Three-topic subscription (system + ingest + balance-worker) with a RecalculateEvent intermediary on the balance-worker topic
decisions.rationale: Separates high-volume ingest fan-out (one event → many entitlements) from the actual ClickHouse balance calculation, enabling independent retry and high-watermark deduplication.

decisions.decision: Recalculator as a struct separate from Worker
decisions.rationale: Batch recalculation jobs (cmd/jobs) reuse the calculation logic without constructing a Kafka-connected Worker.

openmeter/entitlement/driver.purpose: V1 HTTP driver for the entitlement domain: adapts entitlement.Service and meteredentitlement.Connector to Chi handlers via httptransport.HandlerWithArgs with subject-key-based customer resolution. Also owns the shared Parser singleton and MapEntitlementValueToAPI reused by the v2 driver and balanceworker.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs typed triplet
patterns.description: Each handler is a type alias of httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[Request, Response, Params] with Request/Response/Params named immediately above.
patterns.example: type CreateEntitlementHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[CreateEntitlementHandlerRequest, CreateEntitlementHandlerResponse, CreateEntitlementHandlerParams]

patterns.name: Namespace from context only
patterns.description: All decoders call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder; namespace is never a URL/query param in v1.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return request, err }

patterns.name: Customer resolution in the operation closure
patterns.description: Decoder reads only URL params; resolveCustomerFromSubject runs in the operation, then UsageAttribution is set before calling the service.
patterns.example: cust, err := h.resolveCustomerFromSubject(ctx, request.Namespace, request.SubjectIdOrKey); request.Inputs.UsageAttribution = cust.GetUsageAttribution()

patterns.name: Parser singleton for type dispatch
patterns.description: Parser.ToAPIGeneric dispatches on EntitlementType to ToMetered/ToStatic/ToBoolean; never switch on EntitlementType inline.
patterns.example: return Parser.ToAPIGeneric(&entitlement.EntitlementWithCustomer{Entitlement: lo.FromPtr(res), Customer: *cust})

patterns.name: GetErrorEncoder() for domain error mapping
patterns.description: Every handler passes httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(GetErrorEncoder()); new domain errors must be registered there or they fall through to 500.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("createEntitlement"), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(GetErrorEncoder()))

patterns.name: Separate connector fields for base vs metered ops
patterns.description: meteredEntitlementHandler holds entitlementConnector (base queries) and balanceConnector (grant/balance ops); never cross-use them.
patterns.example: h.balanceConnector.CreateGrant(...) for grants; h.entitlementConnector.ListEntitlements(...) for base queries

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementHandler: CreateEntitlement, OverrideEntitlement, GetEntitlement(ById), DeleteEntitlement, GetEntitlementValue, GetEntitlementsOfSubject, ListEntitlements.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateEntitlement passes empty streaming.CustomerUsageAttribution{} to ParseAPICreateInput then overwrites after resolving the customer. ListEntitlements supports both page-based and limit/offset pagination via commonhttp.Union.

key_file_guides.role: MeteredEntitlementHandler: CreateGrant, ListEntitlementGrants, ResetEntitlementUsage, GetEntitlementBalanceHistory; resolveCustomerFromSubject bridges subject key→customer ID.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveCustomerFromSubject checks IsDeleted(); GetEntitlementBalanceHistory returns windowed + burndown history mapped to GrantBurnDownHistorySegment.

key_file_guides.role: Parser singleton (ToMetered/ToStatic/ToBoolean/ToAPIGeneric) and ParseAPICreateInput; MapEntitlementValueToAPI shared by v1/v2 drivers and balanceworker.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ParseAPICreateInput prunes ActiveFrom/ActiveTo (lifecycle fields not accepted via create). MapRecurrenceToAPI is best-effort for non-named ISO durations.

key_file_guides.role: GetErrorEncoder() returns the domain error encoder used by all handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AlreadyExistsError adds conflictingEntityId to response extensions; unregistered domain errors fall through to generic 500.

anti_patterns: Switching on EntitlementType inline in handler closures instead of calling Parser.ToAPIGeneric
Accepting namespace as a query/path parameter — always resolve via h.namespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx)
Using balanceConnector for base entitlement ops or entitlementConnector for grant/balance ops
Adding new domain error types to handler closures instead of GetErrorEncoder()
Calling entitlement service methods with a raw subject key instead of first resolving to customer.ID via resolveCustomerFromSubject

decisions.decision: Subject-key-centric v1 driver with customer resolution inside the operation closure
decisions.rationale: Subject key is the public v1 identifier but customer.ID is required internally; resolving lazily in the operation keeps the decoder stateless.

decisions.decision: Parser as a package-level singleton shared by v1 and v2 drivers
decisions.rationale: Stateless type-dispatch logic is identical across drivers; a singleton avoids repeated construction while organising per-subtype mapping.

openmeter/entitlement/hooks.purpose: Organisational container for entitlement ServiceHook implementations that enforce cross-domain constraints (currently subscription ownership) without creating circular imports. Each child package implements models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] for one external-domain concern.

patterns.name: Embed NoopServiceHook, override only needed methods
patterns.description: Hook structs embed models.NoopServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] so only PreDelete/PreUpdate are overridden; a compile-time var _ models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] = (*hook)(nil) is mandatory.
patterns.example:
type hook struct { NoopEntitlementSubscriptionHook }
var _ models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] = (*hook)(nil)

patterns.name: IsSubscriptionOperation gate first
patterns.description: Any Pre* override calls subscription.IsSubscriptionOperation(ctx) before the annotation check so the subscription service can bypass the guard during its own lifecycle.
patterns.example: if subscription.IsSubscriptionOperation(ctx) { return nil }; if subscription.AnnotationParser.HasSubscription(ent.Annotations) { return models.NewGenericForbiddenError(...) }

patterns.name: Return GenericForbiddenError for annotation guards
patterns.description: Blocked operations return models.NewGenericForbiddenError so the HTTP encoder maps to 403; any other error type breaks status mapping.
patterns.example: return models.NewGenericForbiddenError(fmt.Errorf("entitlement is managed by subscription"))

patterns.name: Constructor accepts a typed config struct
patterns.description: Constructors follow NewXxxHook(cfg XxxHookConfig) XxxHook even when the config is empty, allowing future extension without breaking callers.
patterns.example: func NewEntitlementSubscriptionHook(_ EntitlementSubscriptionHookConfig) EntitlementSubscriptionHook { ... }

patterns.name: Type aliases for hook interface and noop
patterns.description: Each child defines EntitlementXxxHook = models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] and NoopEntitlementXxxHook = models.NoopServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] so callers use a domain-named type.
patterns.example: type EntitlementSubscriptionHook = models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement]

key_file_guides.role: PreDelete/PreUpdate guard blocking mutation/deletion of subscription-annotated entitlements unless called from within a subscription operation; returns GenericForbiddenError (403).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must check subscription.IsSubscriptionOperation(ctx) before HasSubscription. No I/O or DB calls inside hook methods.

anti_patterns: Implementing a new Pre* method without the IsSubscriptionOperation(ctx) check — breaks subscription self-management
Returning a non-GenericForbiddenError for annotation-guard failures — the HTTP layer maps only ForbiddenError to 403
Adding DB calls or business logic inside hook methods — hooks must be pure guards with no I/O
Omitting the compile-time var _ models.ServiceHook[entitlement.Entitlement] = (*hook)(nil) assertion
Importing entitlement.Service (rather than entitlement domain types) — risks import cycles

decisions.rationale: Keeps each cross-domain constraint in its own compilation unit, making it trivial to wire/unwire individual hooks in app/common.

decisions.decision: Embed models.NoopServiceHook rather than implementing all methods
decisions.rationale: Reduces boilerplate and keeps hooks compiling when the ServiceHook interface gains methods.

openmeter/entitlement/validators.purpose: Organisational container for customer.RequestValidator implementations that enforce entitlement-domain pre-mutation constraints on customer operations (e.g. blocking deletion of a customer with active entitlements) without circular imports.

patterns.name: Embed customer.NoopRequestValidator
patterns.description: Validator structs embed customer.NoopRequestValidator so only the relevant Validate* methods are overridden; a compile-time var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil) is mandatory.
patterns.example:
type Validator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; entitlementRepo entitlement.EntitlementRepo }
var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil)

patterns.name: Depend on EntitlementRepo, not entitlement.Service
patterns.description: Validators use entitlement.EntitlementRepo (lighter, cycle-safe) and nil-guard it in the constructor, returning an error if nil.
patterns.example: func NewValidator(entitlementRepo entitlement.EntitlementRepo) (*Validator, error) { if entitlementRepo == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("entitlement repository is required") }; ... }

patterns.name: Return GenericConflictError for blocked operations
patterns.description: Pre-mutation blocking failures use models.NewGenericConflictError so the HTTP encoder maps to 409.
patterns.example: return models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("customer %s still has active entitlements", input.ID))

patterns.name: Use clock.Now() for testable time
patterns.description: All time references use pkg/clock.Now() rather than time.Now() to allow test clock injection.
patterns.example: now := clock.Now(); entitlements, err := v.entitlementRepo.ListEntitlements(ctx, entitlement.ListEntitlementsParams{ActiveAt: lo.ToPtr(now), ...})

patterns.name: Validate input before querying
patterns.description: Each ValidateXxx calls input.Validate() first to reject malformed input before any DB query.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

key_file_guides.role: ValidateDeleteCustomer lists active entitlements at clock.Now() (including soft-deleted ones active at that instant) and returns GenericConflictError if any exist; depends on entitlement.EntitlementRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use clock.Now() not time.Now(). The IncludeDeleted+IncludeDeletedAfter pair is required to catch entitlements soft-deleted after the check. Return GenericConflictError so HTTP maps to 409.

anti_patterns: Importing entitlement.Service instead of entitlement.EntitlementRepo — creates import cycles and a heavier dependency graph
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() — breaks test determinism
Returning a non-GenericConflictError for 'has active entitlements' — the HTTP layer maps only ConflictError to 409
Adding state-mutating or post-mutation logic — validators are read-only pre-mutation guards only
Omitting the compile-time var _ customer.RequestValidator = (*Validator)(nil) assertion

decisions.decision: Depend on entitlement.EntitlementRepo rather than entitlement.Service
decisions.rationale: RequestValidator is called from within the customer service; depending on entitlement.Service would risk import cycles since the service layer imports customer for hooks.
Mirrors the hooks/ layout so each validator can be independently wired/unwired in app/common.
